speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Welcome back, [username]!

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Today, we’re embarking on a quest to make Paneer Chilli Momos.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: I’ll guide you through every step, if you’re about to make a mistake, I’ll catch it before disaster strikes. Ready?

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Let's take a look at the ingredients. Shall we?

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Refined flour
Salt
Sugar
Water

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Oil
Carrots
Cabbage
Light soy sauce

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Green chillies
Ginger
Paneer
Fresh coriander leaves

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Black pepper powder
Vinegar
Green chilli sauce

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Tender coriander stems with roots
Tomato ketchup
Dry Kashmiri red chillies
Onions

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Capsicum
Fried momos
Toasted sesame seeds
Spring onion greens
Coriander sprig

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now let's begin, get all your ingredients ready.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Take your refined flour in a bowl. Add a little salt and a pinch of sugar. Now start adding water slowly — go gradually.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: If the dough suddenly feels sticky or watery — pause. Don’t panic. Just sprinkle a little more flour and continue kneading gently.Smooth and soft is what we want.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Great! Once it comes together, drizzle a little oil on top. Rub it lightly and let the dough rest. Perfect — the foundation of our momos is ready.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now take carrots,cabbage,pinch of salt, a touch of sugar, and little soy sauce

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Let's begin cutting and chopping the veggies

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now take chopped carrots and cabbage in a bowl. Sprinkle a pinch of salt, a touch of sugar

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now add a little soy sauce.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Mix it gently and let the veggies rest for a few minutes.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If your mix starts looking too watery — that means the veggies have released moisture. Good! That’s supposed to happen. Just squeeze the mixture well so that we remove the excess water.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Transfer the squeezed veggies into a clean bowl — nice work.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Add chopped green chillies and chopped ginger to the vegetables.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now crumble your paneer — gently, not aggressively. We want soft crumbs, not paste. Add fresh coriander leaves, a little salt, and some pepper.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If your paneer starts sticking into a paste, stop pressing. Fluff it with your fingers to bring back the crumbly texture.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Mix everything evenly. Perfect — your momo stuffing is ready and smells fantastic.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: In a bowl, add vinegar, soy sauce, green chilli sauce, red chilli sauce, sugar, chopped ginger, chopped tender coriander stems with roots, tomato ketchup, and a splash of water.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Mix it well until it becomes a smooth, shiny sauce.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Looks Yum, doesn't it?

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Wowiee, Yummy!

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If it tastes too strong or too spicy, once it cooks, it will balance out.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Take your rested dough.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Make small lemon-sized balls and roll them into thin discs.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If the edges are thick and the center is thin — rotate your rolling pin around the edges more than the center. If the dough keeps shrinking, give it a few seconds to relax.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Place a spoonful of stuffing in the center. Fold gently, make pleats on one side, and seal it tightly.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If it’s not sealing properly, lightly wet the edge with water and press again. No gaps — or the stuffing will escape when frying.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Heat oil in a kadai. Wait until the oil becomes moderately hot — not smoking, not chilly.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If you drop a momo and it sinks without bubbling — oil needs more heat. If it sizzles too violently — reduce flame immediately.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Now gently drop the momos in.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Fry them until they turn golden and crispy. Beautiful! They look perfect.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Heat a little oil in a wok.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Add chopped dry Kashmiri red chillies — sauté for a few seconds. Add diced onions and capsicum. Toss on high flame — we want crunch, not softness.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If the vegetables start releasing water, increase the heat and stir faster. Crunch is key here.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Add the fried momos and toss everything together quickly on high flame. Make sure each momo gets coated with the sauce beautifully.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Transfer your spicy, glossy Paneer Chilli Momos to a plate.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds, chopped spring onion greens, and place a small coriander sprig on top.

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Let's dig in!

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: It tastes so good!

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: Well done you were good at it

speaker: Nami
dialogue_text: See ya...!
